# DeepVol: Volatility Forecasting with Dilated Causal Convolutions

**Paper**: Moreno-Pino, F. & Zohren, S. (2024). *DeepVol: volatility forecasting from high-frequency data with dilated causal convolutions.* Quantitative Finance, 24(8), 1105–1127.  
**DOI**: https://doi.org/10.1080/14697688.2024.2387222  
**Generated by**: ArXivist pipeline (paper_id: `paper_deepvol_dilated_causal_convolutions`)

---

## What This Paper Does

DeepVol uses a stack of **Dilated Causal Convolution (DCC)** blocks — architecture inspired by WaveNet — to forecast **day-ahead realised volatility** directly from raw high-frequency intraday return sequences. Unlike classical methods (GARCH, HEAVY, HAR), DeepVol requires no preprocessing steps: it ingests raw 5-minute log-returns and learns its own intraday feature extraction via attention-weighted DCC layers.

**Key result**: DeepVol outperforms all 15 baseline models (including HEAVY, HAR, LSTM) on MAE and RMSE on NASDAQ-100 data, with 14.5% MAE improvement over HEAVY.

---

## Quick Start

```bash
git clone <this-repo>
cd paper_deepvol_dilated_causal_convolutions
pip install -e .

# Dry run (validates setup, no training)
python train.py --config configs/config.yaml --dry-run

# Train (uses synthetic data if real data unavailable)
python train.py --config configs/config.yaml

# Evaluate
python evaluate.py --config configs/config.yaml --checkpoint checkpoints/best.ckpt
```

---

## Installation

**pip:**
```bash
pip install -r requirements.txt
pip install -e .
```

**conda:**
```bash
conda env create -f environment.yaml
conda activate deepvol
pip install -e .
```

**Docker:**
```bash
cd docker && docker-compose run train
```

---

## Data Setup

See `data/README_data.md`. Real NASDAQ-100 HF data is **proprietary** (Refinitiv / Bloomberg).  
All scripts fall back to **synthetic data** automatically for testing.

---

## Training

```bash
python train.py --config configs/config.yaml --seed 42
# Debug mode (tiny data, 2 epochs):
python train.py --config configs/config.yaml --debug
```

---

## Evaluation

```bash
python evaluate.py --config configs/config.yaml --checkpoint checkpoints/best.ckpt
```

---

## Expected Results (Table 3, Section 5.3)

| Model       |   MAE |  RMSE | SMAPE |   QLIKE |
|-------------|------:|------:|------:|--------:|
| **DeepVol** | **3.903** | **8.457** | **0.279** | **340.779** |
| HEAVY       | 4.565 | 10.239 | 0.292 | 343.490 |
| LSTM        | 4.441 | 10.032 | 0.295 | 345.465 |
| HAR         | 4.587 | 10.306 | 0.293 | 343.652 |
| martingale  | 5.180 | 11.410 | 0.324 | 747.480 |

---

## Architecture (Section 4.2)

```
raw_returns [B, 1, 78]              ← 78 = 5-min intervals in 6.5hr trading day
    ↓ InputProjection
[B, 32, 78]
    ↓ 2 blocks × 6 DCCLayers        ← dilation = 2^l per layer (1,2,4,8,16,32)
    ↓ SkipConnectionSum [B, 128, 78]
    ↓ BahdanauAttention              ← NOT self-attention
[B, 128]
    ↓ OutputMLP (hidden=64)
sigma2_hat [B, 1]                   ← day-ahead realised variance
```

**Hyperparameters (Table 1):**

| Parameter | Value |
|---|---|
| Kernel size | 3 |
| Num blocks | 2 |
| Num layers | 6 |
| Residual channels | 32 |
| Dilation channels | 64 |
| Skip channels | 128 |
| End channels | 64 |
| Optimizer | Adam, lr=1e-3 |
| Batch size | 512 |
| Epochs | 1000 (early stop patience=50) |
| Loss | QLIKE |

---

## Reproducibility Notes

| Section | Confidence | Note |
|---|---|---|
| DCC layer equations | 0.98 | Explicitly stated (Eq. 24-25) |
| Hyperparameters | 0.99 | All from Table 1 |
| Gated activation | 0.78 | **ASSUMED** tanh×sigmoid (WaveNet style) — not stated |
| Output head MLP | 0.65 | **ASSUMED** 2-layer MLP — architecture not fully specified |
| Input layout | 0.72 | **ASSUMED** flattened 1D sequence |
| Weight decay | 0.80 | **ASSUMED** 0 — not mentioned |
